error.signature: can't find crate — Rust crate name has hyphens but use/extern expects underscores
error.regex: can't find crate|unresolved import.*-|extern crate.*not found

verdict.summary: Cargo allows hyphens in crate names (my-crate) but Rust source code requires underscores (my_crate) for use/extern statements.

dead_ends.action: Use hyphens in use statement matching Cargo.toml
dead_ends.why_fails: Rust identifiers cannot contain hyphens; the compiler parses my-crate as my minus crate

dead_ends.action: Rename the Cargo.toml package name to remove hyphens
dead_ends.why_fails: This changes the published crate name; breaks existing dependents and crates.io registry entry

workarounds.action: Use underscores in Rust source code: use my_crate (Cargo auto-maps hyphens to underscores)
workarounds.how: use my_crate::something;  // Cargo.toml has [dependencies] my-crate = "1.0" but Rust uses underscores

workarounds.action: If renaming is needed, use the [lib] name field in Cargo.toml to set a custom lib name
workarounds.how:
[lib]
name = "my_custom_name"  # explicit lib name separate from package name
